vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on /var/log Directory
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /var/log, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0755 /var/log
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: find -H /var/log/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+ws,o+wt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-ws,o-wt {} \;

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only modifies directory permission bits on /var/log, a standard STIG hardening step. It does not affect authentication, networking, or boot configuration, and will not lock out users or disrupt critical services. The change is straightforward and low‑risk.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential temporary access issues for processes expecting non‑standard permissions; Incorrect command syntax could leave permissions unchanged
